page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with the x86_64 architecture. It features 56 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading, supporting two threads per core. The system is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong multi-core and single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng CPU tests and Passmark CPU evaluations. The memory subsystem performs exceptionally well in specific scenarios, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier and small-block cached read bandwidth also in the top 25%. However, uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average category. The server shows strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing, alongside top-tier performance in text generation for small language models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highcpu-56 offers high compute density and strong processing power per resource unit, though its low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The lack of bundled local storage requires external storage attachments for persistent data. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and medium-scale LLM text generation, while being less optimal for massive database deployments or memory-heavy ETL pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is a compute-optimized server featuring 56 dedicated vCPUs and 112.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. With a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, no bundled local storage, and no GPU, this instance is designed specifically for high-compute workloads. Benchmarks show strong performance in multi-core CPU tests, software compilation, and ray tracing, alongside top-tier memory scores in Passmark and high-efficiency text generation for small language models. However, its low memory-to-core ratio and lack of local storage represent clear tradeoffs for memory-heavy operations. It is highly suited for compute-bound tasks like software compilation, image processing, and text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is a compute-optimized server with 56 dedicated vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is a compute-optimized server with 56 vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, built for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, optimized for CPU-intensive workloads, software compilation, and parallel processing tasks.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 dedicated vCPUs and 28 physical cores.
Provides 112.0 GB of RAM, establishing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Delivers strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and software compilation.
Achieves top-tier memory performance in Passmark and small-model LLM text generation.
Lacks bundled local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring external storage solutions.
Best suited for compute-bound workloads, ray tracing, and image processing.
